detaileddescription.para: A high-frequency, real-time 3D gaze estimation & projecting GDExtension plugin for Godot 4.7+.

listitem.para:
Cross-Platform Gaze Tracking:
Native Desktop & Mobile (macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, Android): Runs an optimized CPU-based ONNX Runtime (CPU/XNNPACK) pipeline for face detection (YuNet) and gaze estimation.
WebAssembly (WASM): Leverages browser-native ONNX Runtime Web (WebGPU/WebGL accelerated) and MediaPipe Face Landmarker JS to process gaze outside the WebAssembly runtime, passing tracking data back to Godot.
Background Thread Inference: All ML model estimation runs in a background worker thread (GazeTrackingPipeline) to keep Godot's main thread smooth and responsive.
Principled 3D Spherical Calibration: Corrects biological user differences using 3D angular biases (pitch/yaw) applied directly to the estimated 3D gaze vector.
Adaptive Smoothing: Implements a 1 Euro Filter to suppress saccadic jitter during steady gaze while preserving low latency during rapid eye movements.

sect2.title: 1. Installation
sect2.para: You can install godot-gaze using one of the following methods:

listitem.para: Download the latest godot-gaze.zip from the GitHub Releases page.
Extract the archive and copy the addons/godot-gaze directory directly into your Godot project's root addons/ directory.
Open your project in the Godot Editor, navigate to Project Settings > Plugins, and enable the Godot Gaze plugin.

sect3.title: B. Local Development Symlinking (For Contributors)
sect3.para:
If you are developing this plugin locally and want to test changes in your own Godot project without repeatedly copying files:
Create a symbolic link from your project's addons directory back to this repository's addon folder: ln-s/path/to/godot-gaze/project/addons/godot-gaze/path/to/your-project/addons/godot-gaze
When you run scons to rebuild any template (e.g. scons platform=macos target=editor), the compiled binaries in project/addons/godot-gaze/bin are instantly available and loaded by your target project.

listitem.para: In your Godot Scene, add a GazeTracker node.

The tracker automatically attempts to guess the camera offsets, screen resolution, and physical display characteristics. You can also customize these properties on the node in the Inspector if needed:
camera_offset: Camera mount position relative to the screen center in millimeters.
camera_tilt: Downtilted angle of the camera in degrees.
screen_size_pixels: Monitor resolution in pixels.
screen_size_mm: Physical display dimensions in millimeters.

Default Calibration Resources: The plugin registers project settings that specify where user calibration resources are automatically loaded:
gaze/calibration/device_calibration_path (defaults to user://calibrations/device_calibration.tres)
gaze/calibration/bio_calibration_path (defaults to user://calibrations/bio_calibration.tres) When GazeTracker initializes, it automatically attempts to load custom .tres resources from these paths, seamlessly applying device and biological corrections.
Model weights (.ort format) are pre-bundled inside the addon's models/ folder.

sect2.title: 3. Basic GDScript Usage
sect2.para: Attach a script to your scene root node to start receiving tracking data:

sect1.title: Web / HTML5 Export Guidelines
sect1.para: When exporting your project to the Web, keep the following considerations in mind:

listitem.para: Automatic Sidecar Export: The Gaze Tracker plugin includes an editor export plugin (export_plugin.gd) that automatically copies gaze_sidecar.js to your HTML5 export folder.

Cross-Origin Isolation Requirements (For Threaded Exports):
Godot's WebAssembly export template supports multi-threading via SharedArrayBuffer. If you enable Thread Support in your Godot Web Export Preset options, the hosting server must serve your game with the following HTTP response headers: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy:same-origin Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy:require-corp
These headers are standard browser security requirements for SharedArrayBuffer (not specific to this plugin). If your hosting environment (e.g. itch.io, GitHub Pages, or corporate intranet) does not allow you to configure these custom headers, you must disable Thread Support in your Godot export preset, which falls back to the non-threaded WebAssembly template.

sect1.title: Developer Guidelines & Compiling
sect1.para: For guidelines on compiling the plugin from source, running tests, or contributing, please refer to CONTRIBUTING.md. For detailed design and math specifications, refer to the Mathematical & Physical Model Guide and the Architecture & Layout section of CONTRIBUTING.md.
